finding: The 'renders populated' content assertion `expect(text.length).toBeGreaterThan(40)` reads innerText of the `gallery-page-<slug>` SECTION, which always contains the gallery chrome (Title=page.path + caption 'gallery-page-<slug> · seeded via mock-API'). Measured: chrome alone is >=46 chars for the SHORTEST surface (46 for 'setup', 68-70 for the others) BEFORE any inner page renders. So the threshold is satisfied by chrome even when the seeded component renders nothing (silent seed failure / empty-state). The header comment claims it proves 'more than just the gallery chrome label' and guards 'rendered only chrome' — it does not. The spec's PRIMARY claim (surfaces render POPULATED) is unverified; only the no-crash + no-console-error checks carry weight. Fix: assert on the inner `[data-gallery-frame]` subtree (or a specific role/heading), not the whole section.

Same vacuous content check as gallery-newly-seeded: `expect(text.length).toBeGreaterThan(40)` on the `gallery-page-<slug>` section, whose chrome caption+Title alone is 46-70 chars for all four gap surfaces (setup/settings-sessions/settings-sandbox/files-detail). A regression where a gap cassette (App.getSetupStatus / Auth.getSessionSettings / CodeSandbox.listRootfsVersions / File.get) stops seeding would leave the page empty-but-not-crashed and the test would still pass green — exactly the class of bug this spec exists to catch.

The console-error filter `!/Failed to load resource/i.test(m.text())` (duplicated in gallery-gap-seed:14) drops ALL failed-resource console errors. Combined with the vacuous >40 content check, a seeded surface whose cassette endpoint is missing (unmocked /api → failed request) could pass green with no populated content. Mitigated: it mirrors the runtime-health gate precedent and page-errors (JS exceptions) are still captured. Note only.

SeededSurfaceFrame's AppErrorBoundary uses `fallback={() => null}` (unlike PageFrame/OverlayFrame which use galleryCrashFallback emitting the `gallery-crash` testid). So a crash inside a SEEDED-class surface renders null with NO gallery-crash marker, making any `getByTestId('gallery-crash')).toHaveCount(0)` assertion ineffective for seeded surfaces. NOT triggered by this branch's 3 specs (all four gap slugs + five new slugs resolve as enumerated PAGE routes using galleryCrashFallback, and TEST-11 overlays use OverlayFrame which does emit the marker), but it is a latent gap for any future seeded-surface crash assertion. Pre-existing in the frame, unchanged by the diff.

Parity spec is sound: baseline (155 slugs) matches the true branch merge-base (482a9cd0) EXACTLY (verified 155/155), the superset check `baseline.filter(s => !registered.has(s))` genuinely catches a dropped/renamed slug, and the duplicate check is real. Reads from the live runtime registry (window.__GALLERY_LIST_ALL_SURFACES__) so a module gallery that throws at import would fail the gallery-root wait. Meaningful, not always-true.

Forward-merge hazard (not a diff defect): the parallel `main` (sidebar-recent-paging) added 3 seeded surfaces — seeded-recent-convos-loaded/error/loading-more — to the central seededSurfaces.tsx entries region that THIS branch deletes/flips to an aggregator. This branch migrated only the 2 that existed at its base (loading/empty). On merging main, those 3 main-side surfaces must be re-homed into chat/gallery.tsx or coverage is silently lost, and the frozen 155-slug baseline won't flag it. Verified: all 155 merge-base slugs migrated with zero drops; the 3 are genuinely main-only.

Migration is VERBATIM. Verified across all ~30 module gallery.tsx: 155/155 merge-base slugs preserved (0 dropped), 282/282 note+title strings present verbatim, 10/10 interaction d.click targets preserved, all 6 hand-authored cassettes (auth/chat/citations/projectDeep/workflow/llmProviders) re-homed to their owning module (authCassette spread + gap seed added), import paths correctly rewritten ./fixtures/*→@/dev/gallery/fixtures/* and ./seeded/helpers→@/dev/gallery/support (no stray relative imports). tsc --noEmit passes clean (exit 0, zero gallery errors) — no dropped import, wrong path, or unmoved helper.

No render-path reactive-proxy reads. Every `Stores.X.*` reference in the module galleries is an ACTION invocation (openDrawer/open/setState) inside a mount-time `open`/`setup` closure, never a reactive `Stores.X.field` read in a render body. There are ZERO inline React component definitions in any module gallery.tsx, and the only `.map()` is a data transform inside a chat setup closure. No Rules-of-Hooks risk.

No a11y defect in the diff. TEST-11 asserts an open overlay portals a role=dialog (mild a11y-positive). The specs don't run axe/AA-contrast on the newly-covered surfaces, but that is the separate runtime-health/Layer-A gate's job (DoD criterion 2), not these functional enumeration specs.
